status: Reusable SQL pattern cards for practical reasoning. Each card is the canonical SHAPE of a high-value BI-SQL move, with the key rule, when/when-not, and the grain/fan-out/null risks to watch. Original retail examples on the fictional schema; not runtime code.

patterns.name: Verify join cardinality before trusting a join
patterns.intent: Confirm the one-side key is unique so the join preserves grain (many:1), not inflates it (many:many).
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT key, COUNT(*) FROM dim GROUP BY key HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;  -- empty result = safe
patterns.key_rule: A dimension join is only many:1 if the join key is unique on the dimension side; verify, do not assume.
patterns.when_to_use: Before any join feeding an additive aggregate, or whenever a SUM/COUNT looks too large.
patterns.avoid_when: The key uniqueness is already enforced by a verified PK constraint you can read from metadata.
patterns.grain_risk: If the one side is not unique the result grain silently changes.
patterns.fanout_risk: high -- this check is the guard against fan-out (SC-011).
patterns.null_count_warning: Null keys match nothing; count them separately.
patterns.target_output: A one-line cardinality verdict (safe many:1 / unsafe many:many) before the join is trusted.

patterns.name: Aggregate at grain, then join (fan-out-safe)
patterns.intent: Compute additive totals without a one-to-many join multiplying rows.
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT p.category, SUM(s.quantity*s.net_price) FROM sales s JOIN product p ON p.product_key=s.product_key GROUP BY p.category;  -- product verified many:1; never join a 1:many table before the SUM
patterns.key_rule: Keep the fact at its grain; bring attributes only from verified-unique dimension rows. If a 1:many table is needed, pre-aggregate it to one row per key first.
patterns.when_to_use: Any revenue/quantity/count rollup that also needs dimension attributes.
patterns.avoid_when: There is genuinely no join (single-table aggregate).
patterns.grain_risk: A 1:many join before the aggregate changes grain and double-counts.
patterns.null_count_warning: INNER join drops null-keyed fact rows; use LEFT join if they must survive.
patterns.target_output: A correctly-grained aggregate; never patched with DISTINCT.

patterns.name: Deterministic deduplication (choose the survivor)
patterns.intent: Keep exactly one chosen row per key with an explicit tiebreak.
patterns.sql_shape: WITH ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY key ORDER BY loaded_at DESC) rn FROM t) SELECT * FROM ranked WHERE rn = 1;
patterns.key_rule: Dedup must choose WHICH row survives via a deterministic ORDER BY; DISTINCT and bare GROUP BY do not.
patterns.when_to_use: Silver/gold dedup, latest-record-per-key, collapsing duplicate loads.
patterns.avoid_when: Whole-row duplicates with no survivor preference (then DISTINCT is fine).
patterns.grain_risk: Without a tiebreak the survivor is nondeterministic.
patterns.fanout_risk: low -- this reduces rows, but verify it does not hide an upstream fan-out.
patterns.null_count_warning: Nulls in the ORDER BY column make the survivor nondeterministic.
patterns.target_output: One explicit survivor per key, reproducible across runs.

patterns.name: Safe anti-join (rows in A with no match in B)
patterns.intent: Find orphans / missing rows without the NOT IN + NULL trap.
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT a.* FROM a LEFT JOIN b ON b.key=a.key WHERE b.key IS NULL;  -- or NOT EXISTS; never NOT IN over a nullable subquery
patterns.key_rule: Use NOT EXISTS or LEFT JOIN ... IS NULL; NOT IN returns zero rows if the subquery contains a NULL.
patterns.when_to_use: Orphan-fact checks, source-minus-gold reconciliation, exclusion lists.
patterns.avoid_when: The compared set is provably non-nullable and small (NOT IN is then equivalent).
patterns.null_count_warning: high -- this pattern exists to avoid the NOT IN null trap (SC-012).
patterns.target_output: The exact set of unmatched rows, with no silent null-driven empty result.

patterns.name: Deterministic running total
patterns.intent: Cumulative sum over an ordered series with a stable result.
patterns.sql_shape: SUM(x) OVER (PARTITION BY grp ORDER BY d ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)
patterns.key_rule: Specify an explicit ROWS frame and a total ORDER BY (with a tiebreak); the default RANGE frame on a non-unique order column is wrong.
patterns.when_to_use: Cumulative revenue, running counts, to-date balances.
patterns.avoid_when: A simple GROUP BY total is what is actually wanted.
patterns.grain_risk: The series must be at one row per ordered unit (often via a date spine).
patterns.null_count_warning: Gaps in the series distort the cumulative line; fill from a spine first.

patterns.name: Period-over-period (YoY / MoM)
patterns.intent: Compare a measure to the same measure one period earlier.
patterns.sql_shape: LAG(metric) OVER (PARTITION BY grp ORDER BY period) -- on a complete, gap-filled period series
patterns.key_rule: LAG reads the previous ROW, not the previous PERIOD; anchor on a complete date spine so 'previous row' equals 'previous period'.
patterns.avoid_when: The series is sparse and not yet spine-filled.
patterns.grain_risk: Sparse periods make LAG compare non-adjacent periods.
patterns.null_count_warning: First period has a NULL prior; guard the delta/ratio (DIVIDE-style).
patterns.target_output: A like-for-like period delta on a complete series.

patterns.name: Date-spine fill (no missing periods)
patterns.intent: Make every period present so trends and windows are contiguous.
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT c.d, COALESCE(SUM(s.amount),0) FROM cal c LEFT JOIN fact s ON s.order_date=c.d GROUP BY c.d;
patterns.key_rule: Drive the trend from a generated calendar LEFT-joined to the fact, not from the fact's own dates.
patterns.when_to_use: Any trend, rolling window, or completeness check.
patterns.avoid_when: The fact is already dense and the range is guaranteed complete.
patterns.grain_risk: One row per calendar unit; verify the spine grain matches the fact bucket.
patterns.fanout_risk: low -- but a non-unique spine row would fan out.
patterns.null_count_warning: Empty periods are NULL pre-COALESCE; decide zero vs carry-forward explicitly.
patterns.target_output: A contiguous period series with explicit zero/filled values.

patterns.name: Row-level reconciliation with EXCEPT (both directions)
patterns.intent: Prove two layers hold identical rows, not just identical totals.
patterns.sql_shape: (A EXCEPT B) returns 0 rows AND (B EXCEPT A) returns 0 rows AND COUNT(*) equal;
patterns.key_rule: Equality needs BOTH directions plus a count check; a one-directional EXCEPT does not prove equality and set-distinct can hide duplicate-count differences.
patterns.avoid_when: Only a sum tie-out is needed (use VP-CONTROLTOTAL, cheaper).
patterns.grain_risk: Project exactly the columns that define row identity.
patterns.null_count_warning: Set ops treat NULLs as equal (unlike =); intended here, but know it.
patterns.target_output: A two-directional equality verdict with matching counts.

patterns.name: Idempotent load via MERGE / upsert
patterns.intent: Make a load safe to re-run with no duplication.
patterns.sql_shape: MERGE INTO target t USING staging s ON t.key=s.key WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE ... WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT ...;
patterns.key_rule: Key the MERGE on a verified-unique business key; re-running must keep row count and control totals identical. Append-only INSERT is not idempotent.
patterns.when_to_use: Every layer-to-layer load that may be re-run.
patterns.avoid_when: A full replace/truncate-load is simpler and acceptable.
patterns.grain_risk: A non-unique merge key matches many and inflates.
patterns.fanout_risk: medium -- guard with a uniqueness check on the merge key (SP-CARDINALITY-CHECK).
patterns.null_count_warning: Null merge keys never match -> rows re-inserted each run.
patterns.target_output: A load whose second run is a no-op on counts and totals.

patterns.name: Multi-level subtotals in one pass (ROLLUP)
patterns.intent: Produce per-level subtotals and a grand total consistently.
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT CASE WHEN GROUPING(region)=1 THEN 'All' ELSE region END, SUM(amount) FROM ... GROUP BY ROLLUP (region, store_key);
patterns.key_rule: Use ROLLUP/GROUPING SETS for subtotals; flag subtotal rows with GROUPING(); never feed subtotal-bearing output into another SUM.
patterns.when_to_use: Reporting/gold extracts that need subtotals and a grand total.
patterns.avoid_when: A single flat aggregate is what is needed.
patterns.grain_risk: Mixed-level rows in one result; a downstream re-aggregate double-counts.
patterns.null_count_warning: Subtotal NULLs are indistinguishable from real NULLs without GROUPING().
patterns.target_output: A labeled multi-level report with unambiguous subtotal rows.
